channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanasha Donna Defends Her Son Against Online Criticism
item.description: Kenyan singer Tanasha Donna, who is also known for her relationship with Tanzanian star Diamond Platnumz, has openly addressed critics targeting her young son, Naseeb Junior. Recently, Tanasha shared ...

item.title: South Africans Protest Over Installation of Igbo Cultural Leader in Eastern Cape
item.description: Civil society organisations and members of the Progressive Forces of South Africa staged a demonstration outside the Nigerian High Commission in Pretoria over the recent installation of an Igbo cultur...

item.title: A Comprehensive Guide to Facelift Recovery and What to Expect
item.description: Recovering from a facelift is a gradual process that extends well beyond the operating theatre. The surgery, known as a full facelift or rhytidectomy, targets sagging skin, jowls, and underlying facia...

item.title: Brooke Bellamy addresses RecipeTin Eats plagiarism claims one year later
item.description: Brooke Bellamy, owner of Brooki Bakehouse, has publicly addressed the plagiarism controversy involving popular cooking blog RecipeTin Eats, a year after the issue arose. The dispute drew widespread at...

item.title: Concerns Raised Over ER Whistleblower Safety, Lobbying Transparency, and Firearms Buyback Shortfalls

item.title: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian Addresses American Public on US-Iran Tensions
item.description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian has addressed an open letter to the American people, urging them to reconsider the rationale behind the ongoing US-Israeli military actions against Iran. In his let...

item.title: UK Prime Minister Seeks Stronger European Partnership Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of the United Kingdom has said that the ongoing war in Iran is causing global instability and making it important for Britain to focus on strengthening economic and defence...

item.title: Rising Fuel Prices Push Up Costs for South African Consumers and Businesses
item.description: South Africans are facing new financial pressures after a sharp increase in fuel prices that took effect on 1 April 2026. Diesel jumped by R7.51 per litre and petrol by R3.06 following disruptions in ...

item.title: Solriamfetol improves alertness in early-morning shift workers with shift work disorder
item.description: A recent clinical trial led by researchers at Mass General Brigham has shown that the wakefulness-promoting drug solriamfetol significantly improves alertness and daily functioning in early-morning sh...

item.title: Stanbic Bank educates Mwanza residents on importance of insurance
item.description: Stanbic Bank Tanzania is making efforts to make people understand insurance better by taking awareness sessions directly to the streets of Mwanza. This activity, held outside the bank’s Nyerere Road b...

item.title: Chicago Bulls Waive NBA Player Following Controversial Anti-LGBTQ Statements
item.description: NBA player Jaden Ivey has been waived by the Chicago Bulls after posting anti-LGBTQ comments on Instagram, citing his religious beliefs. Ivey expressed that God did not intend for same-sex relationshi...

item.title: Young Woman Honors Her Late Parents by Cleaning Their Graves
item.description: A young Kenyan lady, Schollah, recently shared a heartfelt video on social media showing her cleaning her late parents’ graves. The video highlighted the care and respect she still holds for her famil...

item.title: India U20 Women’s Team to Face Japan in AFC Asian Cup 2026 Opener
item.description: The Indian U20 women’s football team, popularly known as the Young Tigresses, are gearing up for their opening match of the AFC U20 Women’s Asian Cup Thailand 2026 against Japan on April 2, 2026, at T...

item.title: Early Flu Season in South Africa Highlights Spread of New Influenza A(H3N2) Sub-Clade K
item.description: South Africa's flu season has started earlier than usual this year, with the National Institute for Communicable Diseases (NICD) reporting low-level transmission since mid-March. The unusual early ons...

item.title: V.S. Ilanthirai appointed as Chief General Manager of BSNL Tamil Nadu Circle

item.title: CPS Provides Early Investigative Advice to Police in Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and Lord Mandelson Inquiries
item.description: The Crown Prosecution Service (CPS) is offering early investigative guidance to UK police forces in connection with inquiries into Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, 66, and Lord Peter Mandelson, 72, over th...

item.title: Pakistan confirms Pak-Afghan border fence remains intact amid Taliban claims
item.description: The Ministry of Information and Broadcasting of Pakistan on Wednesday strongly rejected reports claiming the removal of barbed wire along the Pak-Afghan border. These reports, primarily circulated by ...

item.title: Caribbean island struggles with goat overpopulation threatening native wildlife
item.description: The small Caribbean island of Terre-de-Haut, part of Guadeloupe, is facing a serious environmental issue due to an overpopulation of goats. With roughly one goat per inhabitant, these feral animals ro...

item.title: Atheists Question State House Prayer Events, Citing Constitutional Concerns
item.description: The Atheists In Kenya Society (AIK) has strongly criticised revelations that Christian overnight prayer meetings, known locally as keshas, are frequently held at State House. The concern arose after F...

item.title: Duterte received meals during transfer to ICC custody in The Hague
item.description: Contrary to circulating claims online, former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte was indeed served food during his transfer to The Hague following his arrest on March 11, 2025, by the International ...
